hypothesis: Enhancing steric and electrostatic complementarity with SSTR2-specific ECL2/ECL3 and TM5/TM6 regions by mutating positions 5 and 11 will increase selectivity margin by improving SSTR2 binding while reducing off-target affinity.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis is that SSTR2 selectivity can be improved by disrupting conserved interactions at non-pharmacophore positions. The next iteration will test this by focusing mutations at positions 1, 2, 5, 6, 11, and 12.

proposed_changes.rationale: These positions are non-pharmacophore and are known to contribute to conserved-pocket interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5. Focusing mutations here can disrupt off-target contacts while preserving the pharmacophore core (positions 7-10 and Cys3/Cys14).

These mutations are designed to break conserved hydrophobic or electrostatic interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5 while avoiding disruption of the pharmacophore. For example, introducing charged residues at position 1 or 2 can disrupt off-target interactions.
